profile.markdown:
**Alias:** Wild Engine

**Birthplace:** Planet Varis

**Origin:** Eruditio, Agelta Drylands

**Chinese Name:** 强森 (qiángshēn)

A super cyborg who can transform into a car and lead his teammates into battle.

story.markdown:
> "A super cyborg who can transform into a car and lead his teammates into battle."

Johnson was originally from a planet called Varis, where he worked as a delivery driver. On his world, auto racing was the most illustrious sport, but the major races were almost entirely monopolized by the rich and elite. One day, while on a routine delivery, Johnson became mixed up in a race and even managed to win first place March, to the dismay of the wealthy patrons.

To punish this upstart, they plotted for him to have an accident. While driving home a speeding truck sidelined Johnson's car, mangling his body with the twisted steel of the wreck. Miraculously, he survived, and chose to be integrated with his car to create a cyborg body.

After a successful operation, he had a roaring engine as his new heart, and began racing to satiate his need for speed. As Johnson raced to his heart's content, he kept going faster and faster until he triggered the slip-space device secretly built into his chassis and crossed into the Land of Dawn.

content.markdown:
Johnson was a legend on the world of Varis, a place where speed is everything. Here, the sport of auto racing was monopolized by the rich and elite until Johnson became the first commoner to shatter that mold. But just when he shocked the world by winning his first official race, he vanished without a trace.

The only person who had a clue to Johnson's whereabouts was an old man who claimed to have ridden in his car. According to him, Johnson drove so fast that he tore open the fabric of space and went beyond the stars. However, all of those who have heard the story simply dismissed the old man as being senile.

This old man lived an ordinary life and spent most of it in the slums of the city. He was a construction worker in his youth and helped build many of the city's extravagant sights, including the famous Varis Speedway racetrack. He earned a meager salary for his labor, but enjoyed gazing at the downtown lights and getting lost in their promises of a better life.

Years and years passed, and he grew old. He could no longer work like he used to, but his debts continued to pile up. Visits from debt collectors became increasingly frequent and violent, and one night they came for blood.

The old man ran with everything he had, but he felt like he was sinking in mud. This city that he helped build would surely give him a way out, right? Little did he know, his salvation was heading towards him at 180 miles an hour...

> "Hey, get in the car!"

The old man was suddenly pulled into the passenger seat by a firm grip as his pursuers disappeared in the rear-view mirror. Everything happened so quickly that the old man only saw a flash of light, and thought he was finally going to meet his maker.

When he came to his senses, he realized that the driver was just a kid. A young boy that filled up nearly the whole driver's seat.

This was the old man's first time in Johnson's car. He had spent his entire life in Varis, but he had never seen the city like this; at least not at this speed. Johnson drove like he was possessed, speeding around every corner of the city. They were going so fast that the world outside seemed to be moving in slow motion.

> "My pops always said to be fast!"

The old man didn't know what kind of father this boy had, but the boy spoke of him with a sparkle in his eyes and held those words dear to his heart. For the first time, the old man began to understand why the people of Varis kept saying that speed was passion, and the pinnacle of speed was the limits of the universe. After experiencing such extreme speed, he gained a deeper understanding of the city.

The people were always coming and going in a hurry, and it was all exemplified by the words of Johnson's father: "Be fast!"

The old man did not see the boy for many years after that fateful ride. He returned to the construction site despite being too old and sickly for the work. He looked up just as a pipe came crashing down from the top floors. He closed his eyes and thought he would finally be free of his debts.

Suddenly, there was another flash of light, and a figure, or rather the silhouette of a car sped through and grabbed the old man. For a moment, he could not comprehend what he was seeing...

> "I've gotten faster, right? Hahaha!"

It was Johnson! He's become... half machine, half car? A roaring engine was now Johnson's new heart, and a carbon frame was his body. On both sides of him stood exhaust pipes that looked like bottle rockets. In this world where speed is everything, the old man couldn't think of a more fitting body for Johnson.

But it was tragedy that led to this moment. Johnson and his father had gotten into a terrible car accident, and only Johnson survived the crash. He didn't have the money to pay for expensive prosthetics, so he allowed the doctors to perform an experimental procedure to fuse him with his car.

The old man furrowed his brow. This kid had managed to outrun death. And not once, but twice! Despite his age, he saw in Johnson the true meaning of speed. It felt strange, yet real.

As the old man said goodbye to Johnson for the second time, he had a feeling that they would meet at the edge of the universe someday.

The old man's life went on uneventfully after that, until the day he saw Johnson on a jumbotron, crossing the finish line at Varis Speedway. That kid had performed a miracle! He was the first commoner to win an official race. He became a trailblazer and a beacon of hope for the lower class; the catalyst that will change this world forever.

Excitement surged through the old man's veins, and he began dreaming of becoming like Johnson; to have passion pumped through a roaring engine into his soul.

Johnson did not show up at the podium after his win. When he crossed the finish line, something triggered in his mechanical body and a blinding light appeared ahead like a doorway to another world. And just like that, Johnson, who had just become a legend in his world, vanished into the light.

> "Johnson was my apprentice, you know. Now he's out there among the stars. There! That's him!"

The old man got his wish and became like Johnson, and now lives his life at breakneck speed. In his spare time, the old man would tell stories about his time with Johnson. He still believes that if you can go fast enough, you can leave this mundane world and soar into the cosmos, just like Johnson did.

Johnson, at this time, had just arrived on a strange new world. To him, this just meant new faces to meet, new places to explore, and new speed limits to break.

> "Hey, you with the ponytails. Wanna race?"

The roar of his engine echoed throughout Eruditio.

> "A car transformer and the member of Eruditio Rangers. At the final of "Survival: Nexus", a large number of Eruditio residents gathered at Plaza Circular to watch the game. Johnson and Chou maintain the order of the plaza together. After riot of the Iron Guardians, the Plaza Circular was thrown into chaos. Johnson and the others evacuated civilians while fighting with the Iron Guardians, but were outnumbered. They are in grave peril."

details.markdown: When Johnson's HP drops below 30%, he gains a shield for 10 seconds. The shield value scales with level and Physical Defense, starting at 300 shield and increasing up to around 700 shield at higher levels. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

Johnson throws his wrench toward the target location, dealing magic damage to enemies in its path. Enemies around the landing point are stunned for 0.8 seconds.

- Type: Magic Damage Skill
- Effects: Area of Effect, Crowd Control (Stun)
- Base Damage by level: 120 / 140 / 160 / 180 / 200 / 220
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Cooldown: 8 seconds

Notes:
- This skill is disabled when Johnson transforms into a car.

Johnson raises his shield, continuously dealing magic damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area in the target direction and slowing them by 20%. Each instance of damage also increases the enemy's subsequent damage taken from the skill by 15%, up to 45%. This damage is increased by 60% against creeps. Johnson can cast Basic Attacks and skills for the duration.

- Type: Magic Damage Skill
- Effects: Area of Effect, Slow, stacking damage taken increase debuff
- Duration: 3.5 seconds
- Base Damage by level: 80 / 100 / 120 / 140 / 160 / 180 per hit
- Damage instances: can hit up to 8 times over the duration
- Damage increase per enemy's subsequent damage taken: 0% / 15% / 30% / 45%
- Extra damage vs creeps: 60% increased damage
- Cooldown by level: 10.0 / 9.6 / 9.2 / 8.8 / 8.4 / 8.0 seconds
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Mana Cost: 60 (scales with level)

Notes:
- Electromagnetic Waves can deal up to 8 instances of damage within the duration; the exact interval between hits is undetermined.
- This skill can only be interrupted by high- and ultimate-level control effects.
- The damage increase applied to an enemy counts as a debuff and can be removed by cleansing skills.

Additional Interaction:
- When Johnson is in car form, he has access to the alternate skill "Nitrous" instead of this default version.
Hold the "Nitrous" skill to increase speed for up to 5 seconds, the acceleration stopping immediately once released. This is available while Johnson is in car form.

Johnson transforms into a car and begins accelerating over time. An allied hero can use the associated boarding command when near Johnson to board the car (only one allied hero can ride with Johnson normally). The car will explode upon hitting an enemy hero or obstacle, dealing scaling magic damage to nearby enemies, stunning them for 0.5–1 second (scales with the car's speed before impact), and creating an electrified zone at the location. The zone continuously deals magic damage and slows enemies within.

While in car state, tap the brake control to stop immediately or hold the Nitrous control to continuously gain speed for up to 5 seconds. Johnson's location will be revealed to the enemy for 3 seconds when casting this skill. Johnson's Physical Defense is increased by 10%–30% while the skill is active.

- Effects: Morph, Charge, Area of Effect, Crowd Control (Stun), Physical Defense increase
- Skill Duration: approximately 2 hours, or until he bumps into something
- Electrified Zone Duration: 4 seconds
- Physical Defense Bonus by level: 10% / 20% / 30%
- Min Crash Damage: 300 / 350 / 400
- Max Crash Damage: 450 / 525 / 600
- Sustained Zone Damage: 60 / 70 / 80 per tick
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Cooldown by level: 36.0 / 33.0 / 30.0 seconds
- Mana Costs (crash and zone portions) scale with level, starting around 160–280 mana total

Notes:
- Full Throttle deals damage against Turrets.
- The damage scaling on speed is capped at the top speed achieved without Nitrous; activating Nitrous will not increase the damage further beyond that cap.
- With enough speed, it is possible for Johnson to outdrive incoming turret damage while driving.
- The electrified zone can deal up to 9 instances of damage within the duration; the exact interval between hits is undetermined.
- This skill can only be interrupted by suppression.
- Through certain possession-type hero interactions, it is possible to carry up to two passengers at a time.
- Johnson cannot carry Hanzo's body, only his Hanekage form.

Used while in car form during Full Throttle. Immediately stops Johnson's movement, ending the driving phase and triggering the crash effects based on current speed. Shares the same functional description and effects as the crash portion of Full Throttle.

trivia.markdown: Johnson is one of the most unique heroes not only in Mobile Legends but in the world of MOBA.
The old version of Johnson was one of the characters in *Mobile Battleground - Blitz*.
Johnson's "Jeepney Racer" is a tribute to the Philippines. This special Elite skin turns Johnson into a colorful Philippine jeepney, honoring the country’s iconic public transport.
Johnson's skills and certain other abilities are the only skills in the game that scale with speed-related mechanics.
Johnson's ultimate pairs very well with heroes that have high damage AoE abilities; his most popular pair being Odette thanks to her "Swan Song" ultimate. This is referenced on his Mastery Code.

Johnson in his quote "Autobots? Sounds familiar.", and when transforming into a car using his ultimate ability, are references to Autobots from the Transformers franchise which later became a collaboration skin in the game.

markdown:
# Profile

**Alias:** Wild Engine

**Birthplace:** Planet Varis

**Origin:** Eruditio, Agelta Drylands

**Chinese Name:** 强森 (qiángshēn)

A super cyborg who can transform into a car and lead his teammates into battle.

# Story

> "A super cyborg who can transform into a car and lead his teammates into battle."

Johnson was originally from a planet called Varis, where he worked as a delivery driver. On his world, auto racing was the most illustrious sport, but the major races were almost entirely monopolized by the rich and elite. One day, while on a routine delivery, Johnson became mixed up in a race and even managed to win first place March, to the dismay of the wealthy patrons.

To punish this upstart, they plotted for him to have an accident. While driving home a speeding truck sidelined Johnson's car, mangling his body with the twisted steel of the wreck. Miraculously, he survived, and chose to be integrated with his car to create a cyborg body.

After a successful operation, he had a roaring engine as his new heart, and began racing to satiate his need for speed. As Johnson raced to his heart's content, he kept going faster and faster until he triggered the slip-space device secretly built into his chassis and crossed into the Land of Dawn.

# Side Story

## Legend of Speed on Varis

Johnson was a legend on the world of Varis, a place where speed is everything. Here, the sport of auto racing was monopolized by the rich and elite until Johnson became the first commoner to shatter that mold. But just when he shocked the world by winning his first official race, he vanished without a trace.

The only person who had a clue to Johnson's whereabouts was an old man who claimed to have ridden in his car. According to him, Johnson drove so fast that he tore open the fabric of space and went beyond the stars. However, all of those who have heard the story simply dismissed the old man as being senile.

## The Old Man of Varis

This old man lived an ordinary life and spent most of it in the slums of the city. He was a construction worker in his youth and helped build many of the city's extravagant sights, including the famous Varis Speedway racetrack. He earned a meager salary for his labor, but enjoyed gazing at the downtown lights and getting lost in their promises of a better life.

Years and years passed, and he grew old. He could no longer work like he used to, but his debts continued to pile up. Visits from debt collectors became increasingly frequent and violent, and one night they came for blood.

The old man ran with everything he had, but he felt like he was sinking in mud. This city that he helped build would surely give him a way out, right? Little did he know, his salvation was heading towards him at 180 miles an hour...

## First Ride

> "Hey, get in the car!"

The old man was suddenly pulled into the passenger seat by a firm grip as his pursuers disappeared in the rear-view mirror. Everything happened so quickly that the old man only saw a flash of light, and thought he was finally going to meet his maker.

When he came to his senses, he realized that the driver was just a kid. A young boy that filled up nearly the whole driver's seat.

This was the old man's first time in Johnson's car. He had spent his entire life in Varis, but he had never seen the city like this; at least not at this speed. Johnson drove like he was possessed, speeding around every corner of the city. They were going so fast that the world outside seemed to be moving in slow motion.

> "My pops always said to be fast!"

The old man didn't know what kind of father this boy had, but the boy spoke of him with a sparkle in his eyes and held those words dear to his heart. For the first time, the old man began to understand why the people of Varis kept saying that speed was passion, and the pinnacle of speed was the limits of the universe. After experiencing such extreme speed, he gained a deeper understanding of the city.

The people were always coming and going in a hurry, and it was all exemplified by the words of Johnson's father: "Be fast!"

## Outrunning Death

The old man did not see the boy for many years after that fateful ride. He returned to the construction site despite being too old and sickly for the work. He looked up just as a pipe came crashing down from the top floors. He closed his eyes and thought he would finally be free of his debts.

Suddenly, there was another flash of light, and a figure, or rather the silhouette of a car sped through and grabbed the old man. For a moment, he could not comprehend what he was seeing...

> "I've gotten faster, right? Hahaha!"

It was Johnson! He's become... half machine, half car? A roaring engine was now Johnson's new heart, and a carbon frame was his body. On both sides of him stood exhaust pipes that looked like bottle rockets. In this world where speed is everything, the old man couldn't think of a more fitting body for Johnson.

But it was tragedy that led to this moment. Johnson and his father had gotten into a terrible car accident, and only Johnson survived the crash. He didn't have the money to pay for expensive prosthetics, so he allowed the doctors to perform an experimental procedure to fuse him with his car.

The old man furrowed his brow. This kid had managed to outrun death. And not once, but twice! Despite his age, he saw in Johnson the true meaning of speed. It felt strange, yet real.

As the old man said goodbye to Johnson for the second time, he had a feeling that they would meet at the edge of the universe someday.

## A Beacon for Varis

The old man's life went on uneventfully after that, until the day he saw Johnson on a jumbotron, crossing the finish line at Varis Speedway. That kid had performed a miracle! He was the first commoner to win an official race. He became a trailblazer and a beacon of hope for the lower class; the catalyst that will change this world forever.

Excitement surged through the old man's veins, and he began dreaming of becoming like Johnson; to have passion pumped through a roaring engine into his soul.

Johnson did not show up at the podium after his win. When he crossed the finish line, something triggered in his mechanical body and a blinding light appeared ahead like a doorway to another world. And just like that, Johnson, who had just become a legend in his world, vanished into the light.

> "Johnson was my apprentice, you know. Now he's out there among the stars. There! That's him!"

The old man got his wish and became like Johnson, and now lives his life at breakneck speed. In his spare time, the old man would tell stories about his time with Johnson. He still believes that if you can go fast enough, you can leave this mundane world and soar into the cosmos, just like Johnson did.

## New World, New Speed Limits

Johnson, at this time, had just arrived on a strange new world. To him, this just meant new faces to meet, new places to explore, and new speed limits to break.

> "Hey, you with the ponytails. Wanna race?"

The roar of his engine echoed throughout Eruditio.

## Lethal Nexus Incident

> "A car transformer and the member of Eruditio Rangers. At the final of "Survival: Nexus", a large number of Eruditio residents gathered at Plaza Circular to watch the game. Johnson and Chou maintain the order of the plaza together. After riot of the Iron Guardians, the Plaza Circular was thrown into chaos. Johnson and the others evacuated civilians while fighting with the Iron Guardians, but were outnumbered. They are in grave peril."

# Abilities

## Electro Airbag

When Johnson's HP drops below 30%, he gains a shield for 10 seconds. The shield value scales with level and Physical Defense, starting at 300 shield and increasing up to around 700 shield at higher levels. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

## Impact Wrench

Johnson throws his wrench toward the target location, dealing magic damage to enemies in its path. Enemies around the landing point are stunned for 0.8 seconds.

- Type: Magic Damage Skill
- Effects: Area of Effect, Crowd Control (Stun)
- Base Damage by level: 120 / 140 / 160 / 180 / 200 / 220
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Cooldown: 8 seconds

Notes:
- This skill is disabled when Johnson transforms into a car.

## Electromagnetic Waves

Johnson raises his shield, continuously dealing magic damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area in the target direction and slowing them by 20%. Each instance of damage also increases the enemy's subsequent damage taken from the skill by 15%, up to 45%. This damage is increased by 60% against creeps. Johnson can cast Basic Attacks and skills for the duration.

- Type: Magic Damage Skill
- Effects: Area of Effect, Slow, stacking damage taken increase debuff
- Duration: 3.5 seconds
- Base Damage by level: 80 / 100 / 120 / 140 / 160 / 180 per hit
- Damage instances: can hit up to 8 times over the duration
- Damage increase per enemy's subsequent damage taken: 0% / 15% / 30% / 45%
- Extra damage vs creeps: 60% increased damage
- Cooldown by level: 10.0 / 9.6 / 9.2 / 8.8 / 8.4 / 8.0 seconds
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Mana Cost: 60 (scales with level)

Notes:
- Electromagnetic Waves can deal up to 8 instances of damage within the duration; the exact interval between hits is undetermined.
- This skill can only be interrupted by high- and ultimate-level control effects.
- The damage increase applied to an enemy counts as a debuff and can be removed by cleansing skills.

Additional Interaction:
- When Johnson is in car form, he has access to the alternate skill "Nitrous" instead of this default version.

## Nitrous

Hold the "Nitrous" skill to increase speed for up to 5 seconds, the acceleration stopping immediately once released. This is available while Johnson is in car form.

## Full Throttle

Johnson transforms into a car and begins accelerating over time. An allied hero can use the associated boarding command when near Johnson to board the car (only one allied hero can ride with Johnson normally). The car will explode upon hitting an enemy hero or obstacle, dealing scaling magic damage to nearby enemies, stunning them for 0.5–1 second (scales with the car's speed before impact), and creating an electrified zone at the location. The zone continuously deals magic damage and slows enemies within.

While in car state, tap the brake control to stop immediately or hold the Nitrous control to continuously gain speed for up to 5 seconds. Johnson's location will be revealed to the enemy for 3 seconds when casting this skill. Johnson's Physical Defense is increased by 10%–30% while the skill is active.

- Effects: Morph, Charge, Area of Effect, Crowd Control (Stun), Physical Defense increase
- Skill Duration: approximately 2 hours, or until he bumps into something
- Electrified Zone Duration: 4 seconds
- Physical Defense Bonus by level: 10% / 20% / 30%
- Min Crash Damage: 300 / 350 / 400
- Max Crash Damage: 450 / 525 / 600
- Sustained Zone Damage: 60 / 70 / 80 per tick
- Spell Vamp: 50%
- Cooldown by level: 36.0 / 33.0 / 30.0 seconds
- Mana Costs (crash and zone portions) scale with level, starting around 160–280 mana total

Notes:
- Full Throttle deals damage against Turrets.
- The damage scaling on speed is capped at the top speed achieved without Nitrous; activating Nitrous will not increase the damage further beyond that cap.
- With enough speed, it is possible for Johnson to outdrive incoming turret damage while driving.
- The electrified zone can deal up to 9 instances of damage within the duration; the exact interval between hits is undetermined.
- This skill can only be interrupted by suppression.
- Through certain possession-type hero interactions, it is possible to carry up to two passengers at a time.
- Johnson cannot carry Hanzo's body, only his Hanekage form.

## Brake

Used while in car form during Full Throttle. Immediately stops Johnson's movement, ending the driving phase and triggering the crash effects based on current speed. Shares the same functional description and effects as the crash portion of Full Throttle.

# Trivia

- Johnson is one of the most unique heroes not only in Mobile Legends but in the world of MOBA.
- The old version of Johnson was one of the characters in *Mobile Battleground - Blitz*.
- Johnson's "Jeepney Racer" is a tribute to the Philippines. This special Elite skin turns Johnson into a colorful Philippine jeepney, honoring the country’s iconic public transport.
- Johnson's skills and certain other abilities are the only skills in the game that scale with speed-related mechanics.
- Johnson's ultimate pairs very well with heroes that have high damage AoE abilities; his most popular pair being Odette thanks to her "Swan Song" ultimate. This is referenced on his Mastery Code.
- Johnson in his quote "Autobots? Sounds familiar.", and when transforming into a car using his ultimate ability, are references to Autobots from the Transformers franchise which later became a collaboration skin in the game.

metadata.hook: In a world where speed is everything, Johnson outruns death itself, fuses with his car, and drives so fast he tears open space to reach the stars.
metadata.teaser: On Varis, a boy who became a living car shatters class barriers, cheats death twice, and races so fast he disappears into the cosmos and into Eruditio.
metadata.openingLine: Johnson was a legend on the world of Varis, a place where speed is everything.
